meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Overcome Coding Plateaus
content.seo_description: Break free from basic app limitations. Learn to use a SaaS starter kit with Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade software as a student.
content.answer_block: Stop building 'to-do' lists. A SaaS starter kit provides the production-ready infrastructure—auth, payments, and database schemas—that beginners lack. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you bypass boilerplate fatigue and launch functional, high-value applications that differentiate your portfolio in a competitive job market.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks grinding through tutorials, but your GitHub remains a graveyard of disconnected scripts and unpolished 'Hello World' projects. The gap between a student project and a production-ready SaaS feels like an insurmountable canyon, leaving you stuck in a loop of trivial apps that never see a real user.

It is time to stop reinventing the wheel. Real-world engineering isn't about writing every line from scratch; it’s about orchestrating powerful, pre-built systems. By adopting a professional SaaS starter kit, you shift your focus from mundane plumbing to building unique value, transforming your workflow from a student hobbyist to a serious AI-powered developer.

steps.title: Architecting the Core Infrastructure
steps.description: Establish your foundation by defining the SaaS domain requirements. We use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate structure that handles user authentication and database relationships.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust SaaS foundation using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe. Include pre-configured user authentication, role-based access control, and a scalable database schema for a subscription-based product.

steps.title: Designing the Data Layer
steps.description: Define your schema using Claude Code to ensure type safety and relational integrity. This prevents common bugs that plague beginner projects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational schema for a SaaS platform including tables for Users, Subscriptions, and Projects. Use Prisma syntax and ensure foreign key constraints are correctly defined.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create prisma schema for user subscription billing model'

steps.title: Implementing Authentication Flow
steps.description: Hook up your auth provider to your UI. This step ensures security is handled correctly from day one, rather than as an afterthought.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Supabase Auth middleware to protect private routes and redirect unauthenticated users to the login page.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement middleware.ts for route protection with Supabase'

steps.title: Integrating Payment Logic
steps.description: Bridge the gap between your app and Stripe. This is the most critical step for turning a hobby project into a real SaaS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe webhook handler that listens for checkout session completion and updates the user's subscription status in the database.

steps.title: Iterative Feature Deployment
steps.description: Use Claude Code to build out your unique value proposition. Now that the skeleton is built, you can focus on shipping features fast.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a dashboard component that displays real-time user metrics fetched from the database using server-side rendering.
steps.claude_code_command: claude build 'Create analytics dashboard component with real-time data fetching'

pro_tips: Always use environment variables for keys; never commit secrets to Git, even in private student repos.
Focus on 'Feature Flags' early so you can deploy incomplete code to production without breaking the UX.
Use TypeScript strictly; the compiler is your best friend when debugging complex SaaS state transitions.
Automate your testing with simple unit tests for your API routes to prevent regression during rapid iteration.
Document your architectural decisions in a README.md file—interviewers value the 'why' behind your tech stack choices.
content.next_steps: After completing your MVP, deploy your application to Vercel and write a case study on your portfolio site explaining the technical challenges you solved.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build software that actually scales. Don't let your code sit idle—turn this project into your career launchpad. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
